prompt:
Create a visual work with portfolio cover order centered on specific thematic content. The core object should not be displayed in full, but first compressed into a main mass deviating from the central axis, sinking between a translucent atomized surface layer and a low-saturation background field. The full outline, secondary details, and ordinary descriptions of the object are swallowed by embossed glass particles, tiny refractive displacements, milky atomization, and low-contrast blur, leaving only a clean and clear narrow window cut at the position that best carries thematic identification. This part retains realistic texture, high contrast, and necessary colors, while the rest turns into dark masses, softened outlines, broken edges, and transitions consumed by the background. The clear narrow window should be like a precise gap wiped from the material surface, restrained in size but accurate in semantics, becoming the only strong entrance for viewers. The background is derived from the thematic spatial relationships, material attributes, time direction, and emotional temperature into a large-area low-noise color field, with a light airy feel at the top or outer side and a light-colored breathing zone on one side. The area where the main body is located is pressed into lower brightness. The background does not assume ordinary scene narrative but carries the functions of swallowing edges, carrying white text, and creating distance and memory. Color maintains a proportion between large-area low-saturation structural colors, low-brightness main colors, small amounts of concentrated realistic colors, and white information colors; hues change with the theme, while brightness levels, saturation restraint, and local color value remain stable. The picture uses three layers: clear, semi-clear, and out-of-focus. The clear narrow window is responsible for theme identification, short bold white titles and numbers remain semi-clearly readable, and most of the main body, background, and micro-information at the bottom enter atomized out-of-focus. The text uses modern sans-serif fonts, white or near-white, with short, heavy, bold titles that look like they are printed on the glass and slightly bitten by the surface layer. Corner identifiers, edge dates, numbers, and micro-annotations at the bottom form a metadata coordinate system like work numbers, archive records, and production parameters, serving the reading path without covering the clear narrow window. The entire surface retains visible tactile embossed glass texture, with texture density changing with the brightness of the underlying layer—denser in dark parts, milkier in bright parts, with fine refraction and slight displacement at the edges. All images, objects, and text look unified under the same material. The final image should present a calm, restrained visual cover with a technical archival feel: information is intentionally hidden, and only the most critical parts are released.

Theme: Crazy Thursday KFC 
Ratio: 9:10
